$schema_note: This is a human- and AI-readable master record for the Roll30 project. It is NOT source code and is not consumed by the app at runtime. It exists to give any person or AI instant context on what this project is, how it works, and its full change history.

disclaimer_and_usage_rules.what_this_file_is: A single source of truth describing the Roll30 project: its purpose, structure, how it works, and a running log of every change made to it.
how_the_two_sections_relate: 'project_state' is the DOCUMENT: a fuller description of how the app currently works.
'change_log' is the LIST: a running, append-only history where a new entry is added EVERY time any change is made.
disclaimer_and_usage_rules.priority_rule: THE LIST WINS. If anything in 'change_log' conflicts with anything in 'project_state', the 'change_log' is correct and 'project_state' is simply not yet fully updated. Always trust the most recent relevant 'change_log' entries over 'project_state'.
instructions_for_any_ai: 1. Read 'disclaimer_and_usage_rules' and the FULL 'change_log' before acting on this project.
2. Treat the newest 'change_log' entries as the current truth. Where they contradict 'project_state', follow the log.
3. Every single time you make ANY change to the project (code, data, structure, config, GitHub, anything), APPEND a new entry to 'change_log' with a medium-sized summary. Never silently skip this.
4. Do NOT rewrite or delete 'project_state' or prune old 'change_log' entries on your own. Only do a full rewrite / cleanup when the user EXPLICITLY tells you to update the whole file.

5. When the user says to update the whole file: rebuild 'project_state' so it fully matches reality, remove/fix outdated info, then CLEAR the 'change_log' back to an empty list and reset 'change_log_next_id' to 1. Bump 'meta.master_version'.
6. Keep entries factual and concise. Use absolute dates (YYYY-MM-DD), not 'today' or 'yesterday'.

project_state.purpose: A deployed web app that browses the complete Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition SRD reference data as a file-directory explorer, behind a simple password gate.
project_state.current_status: LIVE and deployed. Public GitHub repo, auto-deployed to GitHub Pages on every push to main. Site is protected by a Supabase-backed IP password gate.
project_state.how_the_app_works: A single-page app. On load it fetches the visitor's public IP (via api.ipify.org) and asks the Supabase function is_ip_authorized(); if that IP has unlocked before, the user is admitted automatically. Otherwise a neon lock screen prompts for a password. The password is checked SERVER-SIDE by the Supabase function authorize_ip(pw, client_ip) and is NEVER included in the shipped browser bundle. On a correct password the IP is recorded so future visits from it skip the prompt. Once past the gate, the UI is a file explorer: the home screen shows one folder card per SRD category (emoji icon + item count); clicking a folder lists its entries; clicking an entry opens a collapsible, colour-coded JSON viewer. There is also a breadcrumb trail and a live search box.

tech_stack.styling: Tailwind CSS v4 (via @tailwindcss/vite), neon/glassmorphism theme, animated aurora background
tech_stack.backend: Supabase (Postgres + PostgREST RPC) for the password gate
tech_stack.hosting: GitHub Pages, deployed by GitHub Actions

supabase.schema: Table public.authorized_ips (ip text PK, created_at). RLS enabled with NO direct anon/authenticated access. Two SECURITY DEFINER functions: is_ip_authorized(check_ip)->boolean and authorize_ip(pw, client_ip)->boolean (password lives only inside this function).
supabase.password_note: The gate password is 'MarceloIsFranklyAmazing'. It exists ONLY in the authorize_ip DB function, never in the client bundle.
supabase.gate_caveat: Soft gate only: public IPs are shared behind NAT and change over time, so it can auto-admit others on the same network and occasionally re-prompt. Not suitable for truly sensitive data.
supabase.IMPORTANT_do_not_touch: There is a SEPARATE unrelated Supabase project named 'chara' (ref uilqxmsllyugxuzsxwkx) holding chara_* tables. The Roll30 gate lives ONLY in eujhtcnnjtwsthscdfqk. Never modify the chara project for Roll30 work.

structure.vite.config.ts: Vite config; base is '/Roll30/' to match the GitHub Pages path.
structure.src/gate.ts: Supabase RPC client + public-IP lookup (uses the Roll30 project's public publishable key).
structure.public/data/: Copy of the 25 SRD JSON files served by the app, plus a generated manifest.json.
structure.data/: Original downloaded 25 SRD JSON files kept at repo root.
structure..github/workflows/deploy.yml: GitHub Actions workflow: builds and deploys to GitHub Pages on push to main.
structure..gitignore: Ignores node_modules/, dist/, and OS/temp junk.
structure.PROJECT_MASTER.json: This file — master record and change log.

data_inventory_counts.note: 25 categories, ~2,317 total entries (per the generated public/data/manifest.json on 2026-07-21). Biggest: features (407), magic-items (362), monsters (334), spells (319), equipment (237).

project_state.how_to_run_locally: From C:\Users\Marcelo\Documents\Roll30 run 'npm install' (first time) then 'npm run dev'. Pushing to main auto-builds and redeploys to GitHub Pages.

tooling.github_cli: GitHub CLI 2.96.0, authenticated as aomarco (with 'workflow' scope), wired into git via 'gh auth setup-git'
